name: Ulyanovsk Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: purity, peace and the snowy winters

description: A horizontal bicolour of white over a narrow blue stripe at the foot, with the coat of arms of Ulyanovsk Oblast (a gold crowned pillar on blue) placed in the upper hoist.
history: The flag of Ulyanovsk Oblast was adopted on 3 September 2004. The arms reproduce those of the historical Simbirsk Governorate; the city of Simbirsk was renamed Ulyanovsk in 1924 in honour of Vladimir Ilyich Lenin (Ulyanov), born there.

features.symbolism: a broad white field over a narrow blue stripe at the foot, with the regional arms in the upper hoist.
a gold pillar (column) topped by a crown on a blue field, the historic arms of Simbirsk Governorate (Ulyanovsk's pre-Soviet name).
